Job Title: Personal Financial Consultant - Global Consumer Financial Services
Job Description: Personal Financial Consultant - Global Consumer Financial Services OCBC opens doors to new opportunities.Start your career with OCBC as a Personal Financial Consultant!At OCBC we are constantly on the lookout for exceptional individuals to join our team. As Singapore’s longest established local bank, we have a global network of more than 450 branches and representative offices in more than 15 countries and territories. Most importantly, we invest in our people through our programmes that develop them on both professional and personal levels. If you have passion, drive and the will to succeed, rise to the challenge today! What is it like being a Personal Financial Consultant at OCBC?As a Personal Financial Consultant, you will be based at one of our island-wide Branches and will provide comprehensive, one-stop financial advisory services to customers through the active promotion and cross-selling of our full range of wealth management products that include insurance, loans, unit trusts and structured deposits. With an emphasis on consultative selling to meet customers’ needs, you will also proactively build and manage long-term customer relationship and provide good after-sales service to them.To help you succeed in your role, you will join our structured training program during the first 6 weeks of your career with us. Thereafter you will also have at least 5 training days every year to help you further develop your career and keep your skills sharp and up-to-date with the latest developments in the industry. As a successful Personal Financial Consultant, you will have the option to take your career forward by either pursuing our Management Route, on the path of becoming a Branch Manager one day, or the Sales Route, growing into a Senior Relationship Manager working with premier private clients. Plus, as a top performer, you will be invited to trips, visiting great locations across the world from Paris, Barcelona to London and many more.QualificationsThe successful candidate should be able to demonstrate the following selection criteria: Preferably has a Degree or a Diploma with at least 2 years of relevant retail banking sales experience.Fresh grads with no prior experience are also welcome to apply. Results-oriented go-getter who works well under pressure.Prepared to have flexible working hours that may include working on weekends.Candidates with more than 2 years of relevant financial advisory experiences and proven sales records will be considered for Senior Personal Financial Consultant position.

This is not a typical 9–5 job, and it’s definitely not a typical career path. This is an opportunity for ambitious, driven young individuals who want more — more freedom, more income, and more control over their future.As a Wealth Manager, you’ll be building a high-earning, future-proof career in finance while enjoying real flexibility over your time. This role is designed for people with a burning passion for success, personal growth, and helping others make smarter financial decisions. If you’re motivated, people-focused, and hungry to build something meaningful, you’ll thrive here.You won’t be clock-watching or stuck behind a desk all day. Instead, you’ll have the freedom to manage your own schedule and design your working day around your lifestyle. Whether you’re balancing studies, travel, family, or personal goals, this role rewards results, not hours worked. Performance matters — and when you perform, you’re rewarded.And yes, the earning potential is very real. This is a high-income opportunity with uncapped upside. Your earnings are performance-based, meaning the harder and smarter you work, the more you earn. There is no ceiling here — top performers build exceptional incomes and long-term financial security. Your ambition directly determines your success.In this role, you’ll work closely with clients to help them plan, grow, and protect their wealth. You’ll build lasting relationships, develop your own client base, and gain hands-on experience in real-world wealth management. Along the way, you’ll sharpen your communication skills, business mindset, and financial expertise while building a career with genuine ownership and long-term potential.You don’t need years of experience to apply. What matters most is mindset. We’re looking for individuals who are ambitious, coachable, driven, and eager to learn. If you’re passionate about finance, personal development, and achieving above-average results, this opportunity was built for you. Graduates, career starters, and motivated career changers are all encouraged to apply.You won’t be doing this alone. Full training, ongoing development, and mentorship from experienced professionals are provided, along with a supportive, high-performance environment that pushes you to be your best. If you’re willing to put in the effort, you’ll be given the tools and support to succeed.If you’re tired of income limits, rigid schedules, and careers that don’t excite you, this is your chance to do something different. A career as a Wealth Manager offers flexibility, financial freedom, and the opportunity to build a future on your own terms.This isn’t just a job — it’s a career move that can change your life.

Job Title: 反欺诈调查经理 | Fraud Investigation Manager
Company: HAIER SINGAPORE INVESTMENT HOLDING PTE. LTD.
Job Description: 公司背景 | Company BackgroundHaier 是一家全球化、多元化经营的集团型企业，业务覆盖多个国家和行业，持续推进国际化与规模化发展。随着业务复杂度和跨区域经营不断提升，集团对廉洁合规、风险防控及反舞弊治理提出了更高要求。为进一步强化集团反舞弊体系建设，提升重点领域和平台的风险识别与治理能力，支持业务长期、合规、健康发展，特设立本岗位。Haier is a diversified global enterprise with operations across multiple countries and industries, continuously advancing international expansion and business scale. Job Title: Assistant Manager, Research Office (2 years contract)
Job Description:
Overview
The key role of the position would be to manage compliance check and conduct monitoring of Investigator Initiated Studies/Trials primarily within NUH and NUHS institutions (if necessary). The check is to ensure that Principal Investigator and their study teams are compliant with regulatory guidelines, ethics committee requirements and institutional policies.
Responsibilities
Conduct Monitoring Visits

Ensure that the coordination of monitoring visits to trials/studies that are assigned to are initiated in accordance with NUHS Research Office’s monitoring framework.
Provide guidance to Principal Investigator (PI) and study team to ensure that the study conduct is managed in alignment with the ethics and regulatory (if any) approved protocol and institution policies.
Generates the monitoring visit reports within the stipulated timelines set by the NUHS Research Office’s Monitoring Visit Work Instructions.
Provide guidance to PI and study team in reporting non-compliance if any was detected during the monitoring visit.
Ensure that the reporting is done in a timely manner as per the required timelines set by institution, ethics committee and regulatory.
Provides attention to details while executing monitoring visits. This is critical to assess the quality and integrity of the study being monitored.

Conduct research data governance audits

Provide guidance to PI and study team to ensure research data collected is managed in alignment with the ethics approved protocol and institution data policies.
Conduct on average 2 audits monthly with the generation of the visit report within 14 working days. To also provide the study team with timely advice on remedial action to be taken to address lapses in data management. 

Conduct Risk Assessment for Clinical Trials

Ensure that the safety and its management are described adequately in the ethics application/protocol.
Provide an assessment to guide the NUHS Research Office team in the recommendation for endorsement.
Provide safety inputs and justifications if the assessment requires the Principal Investigator (PI) to form a Data Safety Monitoring Committee.

Research Education

Ensure that the PI and study team are adequately trained and are familiar with ICH-GCP requirements, CITI training programme and institutional policies.
Provide inputs in the development of training materials for Clinical Research Coordinator training.
Provides commitment and participates in the CRC training activity.

Oversight of the NGEMR activities for research in NUH

Ensure that applicable guides/policies are developed to manage the research component on NGEMR platform.
Ensure that research personnel are adequately trained in managing the NGEMR platform (for research).

Manage Data Verification

Ensure that the research data is collected is in accordance with the ethics and regulatory (if any) approved protocol.
Ensure that the research data is managed in compliance with institution data governance policies.

Communication

Ensure that good communication with all parties involved in a trial/study is in place.
Ensure that trust and support to study team leads in the effective management of research sites. This is beneficial to monitor in managing trial/study compliance, productivity, and collaboration in order to improve trial/study related outcomes. 

Others
Support the Research Office Director and Managers as assigned or ad-hoc, including providing administrative support with research proposal submissions, co-review of research non-compliances and related administrative or logistical duties.
Requirements

Good Degree in Life Sciences or related field.
Have at least 2 - 4 years relevant experience in research management/administration or related industry experience - particularly in compliance related matters (knowledge of scientific principles and protocol design).
Strong problem-solving and good communication skills (verbal and written), and ability to adapt to fast-moving situations, are required in order to manage interactions with researchers, study teams and management.
Experience in data management will be an advantage.
Team player.
Understanding of research regulations and ethics review board requirements.
Detail orientated.

The Care Transition Coordinator serves as a vital bridge between acute hospitals and community care settings, working to ensure discharged patients from inpatient wards, outpatient clinics, and Emergency Departments are appropriately placed in primary or community care facilities such as General Practioners, Family Medicine Centres and Polyclinics.
This role involves collaborating with clinicians and clinic managers to identify suitable patients for community placement, enrolling them with primary care providers, facilitating seamless care transfers in both directions when necessary, and maintaining comprehensive repositories of community healthcare resources.
The coordinator also works extensively with various healthcare stakeholders including Specialist Outpatient Clinics, Community Health Teams, NHG Population Health and Ministry of Health to produce regular administrative reports on patient indicators, whilst providing patients with essential information and serving as the key liaison for accessing available primary and community care resources.

Personal Attributes

Key ResponsibilitiesData Analytics & Performance Monitoring

Analyze healthcare utilization and clinical data to identify opportunities for improvement.
Develop metrics and dashboards to monitor clinical outcomes and healthcare service utilization.
Collaborate with stakeholders to ensure dashboards and indicators meet operational and strategic needs to drive Value-Based Care in NHG Health.

Stakeholder Engagement

Support the Assistant Director in leading stakeholder engagement and driving strategic planning for Value-Based Care initiatives across NHG.
Partner with external stakeholders, including the Ministry of Health (MOH), Synapxe, and other key organizations, to align processes and resource planning with VBC strategies.

Reporting & Administrative Support

Support the team to drive stakeholder engagement to accelerate VBC implementation.

Drive improvements through data analytics, including analyzing patients’ utilization and creating condition-specific dashboards to monitor improvements. (Approx. 40% of time)

Optimize resource utilization and ensure cost-effectiveness.
Develop and maintain a financially sustainable value delivery system.
Benchmark NHG’s performance nationally and internationally and encourage sharing of best practices. (Approx. 20% of time)


Conduct data analysis and leverage relevant resources to identify opportunities that deliver meaningful outcomes for patients and providers. (Approx. 20% of time)
Prepare regular quarterly reports, meeting papers, and presentation materials for senior management and MOH. (Approx. 10% of time)
Undertake any other duties assigned by the Supervisor. (Approx. 10% of time)

Familiarity with data tools (e.g., Excel, SQL, Tableau, Python, or R) is a plus.
Willingness to learn and develop technical skills.

Improvement-Oriented Mindset
Strong Analytical Skills
Numerical Aptitude
Good Communication and Interpersonal Skills

Medical Statistician / Statistician
JOB SUMMARY
To provide statistical leadership and expertise in the design, execution, analysis, and reporting of clinical and translational research projects spearheaded by TTSH Rehabilitation Centre and Institute of Research Excellence (IREx).
The Medical Statistician will oversee and support data management, statistical planning, and analytical activities across multi-centre, multidisciplinary research programmes, and work closely with programme leads, investigators, clinicians, and research staff to ensure high-quality, ethical, and timely research outputs.
MAIN DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES
SPECIFIC
Work with multidisciplinary research teams (researchers, clinicians, coordinators, IT and bioinformatics staff) in the conduct of research studies by:

Providing statistical input into study design, including sample size calculations, data analysis plans, and research protocols for clinical research studies and trials
Designing, developing, and maintaining research databases (e.g. REDCap and other clinical databases) to support data capture, coding, cleaning, and analysis
Performing statistical and computational analysis of clinical and research data in an ethical and timely manner, and interpreting findings to support scientific objectives
Preparing clear and concise statistical reports, tables, figures, and visualisations to communicate results to investigators and collaborators
Conducting data quality control checks to identify and resolve inconsistencies, missing data, and anomalies
Supporting programme-level and project-level data analysis planning, including interim and final analyses
Contributing to the preparation of scientific manuscripts, grant reports, and presentations to funding agencies, institutional stakeholders, or collaborators
Coordinating with software engineers, bioinformaticians, and IT teams on database development, electronic case report forms (eCRFs), and clinical system enhancements
Providing guidance and mentorship to research staff, coordinators, and students in biostatistics, data analysis, and related disciplines
Ensuring that statistical and data management practices comply with Good Clinical Practice (GCP), regulatory, ethical, and institutional requirements

GENERAL

Attend research meetings and contribute statistical input to project discussions and decision-making
Support administrative and documentation needs related to data management and analysis
Assist in building a collaborative and collegial research environment within IREx and partner institutions
Perform other duties as directed by the Reporting Officer, Programme Leads, Principal Investigators, or Head of Department

JOB REQUIREMENTS
EDUCATION

Bachelor’s Degree in Statistics, Computer Science, Information Management, Science, or a related discipline
Master’s Degree or PhD in a relevant field is preferred

EXPERIENCE / SKILLS

Experience in clinical research, healthcare research, or biomedical data analysis
Proficiency in MS Office applications (Word, Excel, Access) and clinical research database systems such as REDCap
Working knowledge of statistical and clinical database platforms (e.g. SAS, Oracle Clinical, EDC, CTMS)
Working knowledge of SQL and programming, with interest in adopting new analytical and computational technologies
Knowledge of Good Clinical Practice (GCP), clinical research processes, and regulatory requirements
Strong analytical, organisational, and problem-solving skills
Excellent verbal and written communication skills to engage effectively with clinicians and researchers
Ability to translate clinical or biological questions into statistical or computational problems
Meticulous, detail-oriented, and committed to research quality and integrity
Strong interpersonal skills with ability to work independently and as part of a multidisciplinary team
Demonstrated initiative, ability to manage multiple projects concurrently, and meet deadlines
Willingness to provide training and guidance in biostatistics or related areas

Job Title: Therapists x Health & Wellness x Bio-resonance
Job Description: About the roleOptimal Health Group is seeking passionate and ambitious therapists (from graduates and mid-career switch candidates to experienced senior therapists) to join our fast growing team in the Orchard Central Region in Singapore. In this full-time role, you will be responsible for providing diagnostics/assessments as well as high-quality natural therapies to our customers. We offer a range of benefits including:Competitive salary and monthly incentive + annual performance-based bonusesFull Training Program for assistants/trainees looking to develop into Therapists with a specialisation in Bioresonance (a fast growing sector to address modern day diseases)Additional on-job training of the various technologies and treatments availableOpportunities for professional development and career advancement - including business travel and secondment's to other Optimal Health locations in Asia and Middle EastFlexible work arrangements (weekday/weekend) to maintain a healthy work-life balance ( no long 12 hour shifts) but regular working hours across 5 day working weeks with team rotations.Access to our own range of natural health products and servicesMedical BenefitsRemuneration Packages & Career opportunitiesWill consist of competitive base between 2800- 3500 SGD as well as strong incentive programsFor senior therapists , bases will extend beyond this salary band with generous incentive programs.Depending on the level of experience both the base and incentive programmes will be calibrated to ensure packages are suitable to the candidate's with a focus on personal development and career advancement.For the right candidates balancing family responsibilities , we are also open to part-time shift arrangements. We promote equal opportunities to all.We want all our staff to feel valued and will benchmark our staff appropriately to industry standards.Where you will be workingYou will be working in the middle or Central Orchard at 111 Somerset , know as a Centre of Excellence for Wellness, Aesthetics and Medical Suites. 111 Somerset is a popular , high traffic, busy mall location with lots of trendy cafes , extensive and affordable places to each lunch , from local delights, to authentic asian cuisines , western /international and halal, vegetarian restaurants all located in the building. The building also has a green foyer located at Level 5.The building is located 2 minutes walk from Somerset MRT , with dual road (Exeter Rd, Somerset Rd) drive way and car park access for taxis and bus transport.The centre itself is spacious modern with window views and is professionally furnished to enable therapists and customers to enjoy the space during consultations and therapies.About usOptimal Health Group is a leading provider of natural and complimentary healthcare solutions. With a focus on integrative and holistic care, we are committed to helping our clients achieve optimal health and wellbeing. Our team of experienced professionals work together to deliver personalised, evidence-based therapies and education programs that empower our customers to take control of their health.We have been operating since 2014 and the founders have personally benefited from these holistic therapies and technologies and believe in building a platform to impact many lives not only in Singapore , but regionally and beyond.We are also exclusive distributors of the technologies we use in-centre in Asia and the Middle East - being the only certified parties to train, practise and service the precision technologies being used. We have quickly grown into a top distributor globally, further enabling us to reinvest into the development of our therapy practise , we we believe strongly in practising what we preach.This enables candidates to obtain qualifications and accreditations that will support them with the health/wellness career path both in Singapore and in the other 90 countries these technologies and therapies are practised.www.optimalhealthgroup.sgIf you're passionate about natural therapies and believe in the power of holistic wellness, we'd love to hear from you. Apply now to join our team and start making a difference in the lives of our clients.

What the role isThe 3-Year Emergency Medical Technician (EMT) contract position with the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) presents an exceptional opportunity for individuals seeking to gain invaluable medical experience as part of the esteemed Life Saving Force. This role offers an exclusive pathway for passionate individuals to immerse themselves in frontline emergency response scenarios.Applicants who hold a valid Class 3 Driving License, coupled with academic credentials such as ‘N’-Levels, 'O' Levels, NITEC, Higher NITEC, or Diploma qualifications, and aspire to contribute to the Lifesaving Force upon graduation, will find the 3-Year EMT position to be an attractive proposition. This career trajectory allows individuals to concurrently engage in meaningful work, earn a competitive salary, and delve into the realm of life-saving endeavours.Upon joining the SCDF, officers can expect to secure stable full-time employment accompanied by a compelling remuneration package. They can also stand to earn between $2,940 to $4,370, inclusive of additional monthly frontline allowances.What we are looking forThe eligibility criteria for the 3-Year EMT Contract role include:Possesses Class 3/3A Driving License with more than 1-year validity, as successful applicants will be required to drive an ambulance that requires either a Class 3 or 3A license.GCE 'N' Level and above.Applicants with NS obligations must be medically classified as PES C2 or above.For Pre-enlistees, there will be a 5-year Minimum Term of Engagement (MTE) bond in lieu of the National Service’s obligation.As valued members of SCDF, employees stand to benefit from a range of perks and privileges, including:Competitive salary range, inclusive of frontline / shift allowanceAnnual incrementsPerformance bonuses14 to 18 Days of Vacation Leave (inclusive of other leave types)Medical and Dental SubsidiesYearly $700 learning and development subsidies to better support MHA’s officers’ professional and personal growth, health and well-being.Employee Welfare BenefitsGroup Insurance CoverageVocational Section Commander CertificationEmergency Medical Technician CertificationEmergency Vehicle Ambulance Driving CertificationSign On BonusSuccessful applicants will receive a $6,000 sign-on bonus (subject to eligibility), with officers also having the chance to secure full-time employment and an additional $24,000 sign-on bonus (subject to eligibility) upon emplacement as a Fire and Rescue Specialist or Paramedic Specialist.Embracing this role not only offers professional growth opportunities but also enables individuals to make a tangible difference in emergency medical care provision. Join us in safeguarding lives and property, while embarking on a fulfilling career journey with SCDF.What you will be working onOfficers enrolled in this course will join the Singapore Civil Defence Force (SCDF) as an Emergency Medical Technician-Driver, entrusted with the vital mission of preserving lives and property in diverse emergency scenarios while operating ambulances. Within this capacity, their duties entail swift response to medical incidents and other rescue operations, leveraging their training to deploy essential medical procedures and administer aid to individuals in distress.Embracing a role within SCDF not only signifies a commitment to community service but also presents a profound opportunity to contribute significantly by safeguarding lives and property amid emergencies.As a 3-Year EMT Contract Officer, individuals can anticipate a structured career trajectory that encompasses rigorous training in Vocational Section Commander and Emergency Medical Technical skills. Upon successful completion of these training modules, officers will be stationed at a designated fire station.For more information, please visit the following linksSCDF EMT - 3 Year Contracthttps://www.scdf.gov.sg/join-us/uniformed-positions/emergency-medical-technician-(3-year-contract)
